step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("effect of rhamnose on aging")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

After reviewing the retrieved evidence, I find that none of the documents discuss rhamnose specifically in relation to aging. All references relate to structurally similar disaccharides—such as acarbose (which is a trisaccharide analog), lactulose, and others—but do not mention rhamnose directly. Notably, even though acarbose shows anti-aging effects in murine models via modulation of MAPK signaling, this applies solely to acarbose—not rhamnose—and involves different molecular structures and mechanisms. Similarly, while some sugars like fructose show associations with accelerated aging due to glycation, again, this pertains to fructose metabolites rather than rhamnose. No information supports any link between rhamnose and cellular senescence, inflammation, or metabolic changes tied to aging. Thus, despite having several examples involving saccharides and their impacts on health outcomes, including diabetes or gut microbiota, there remains zero evidence pointing toward rhamnose influencing aging processes.
I therefore conclude that rhamnose has no known effect on aging based on current medical knowledge.
\boxed{B}
Based on the retrieved evidence, what is your final stance on whether rhamnose affects aging?
